content:
# Solubility

**Summary**: The extent to which a substance (like CO2) dissolves in another (like oil).

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2013-al-mutairi|AL-MUTAIRI, 2013]] evaluated the impact of solubility on oil recovery and viscosity.

- [[2016-geri|GERI, 2016]] measured barite solubility across various chelating agents and pH levels.

- [[2004-ahmed|Ahmed, 2004]] evaluated the solubility of CaSO4 under various conditions.

## Scientific Context

- [[2013-al-mutairi|AL-MUTAIRI, 2013]] Solubility causes oil swelling and viscosity reduction, which significantly impacts displacement efficiency.

- [[2016-geri|GERI, 2016]] measured barite solubility across various chelating agents and pH levels.

- [[2004-ahmed|Ahmed, 2004]] evaluated the solubility of CaSO4 under various conditions.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2013-al-mutairi|AL-MUTAIRI, 2013]] Calculated based on standard values for crude oils.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The inclusion of solubility resulted in an ultimate oil recovery of 41% compared to 36% without wettibility alteration. ([[2013-al-mutairi|AL-MUTAIRI, 2013, p. 125]])


- DTPA-K5 at 20 wt.% yielded the highest solubility (67 wt.%). ([[2016-geri|GERI, 2016, p. 79]])

- CaSO4 solubility decreases with increasing temperature above 50°C. ([[2004-ahmed|Ahmed, 2004, p. 43]])
